data.title: S&P 500 Rebounds on Uplifting Jobless Claims Data
data.description:
Following two consecutive lower closes, the S&P 500 (SPX) is set to open Thursday's trading session higher on new labor market data.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For the week ended January 9, initial jobless claims fell by 9,000 to 198,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, decreased by 19,000 to 1.884 million, also below expectations of 1.897 million.

*This is a developing story. Stay tuned for additional updates.*

data.title: ASML Stock Surged on an Analyst Upgrade & Price Target Increase
data.description:
ASML (ASML) stock soared on Thursday after analysts weighed in on the advanced semiconductor equipment systems company following a strong earnings report from one of its customers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Let's start with a dive into the latest analyst updates for ASML stock.

  • KGI Securities analyst Leon Chen upgraded ASML stock to an Outperform rating from a Hold rating and set a $1,415 price target for the shares, suggesting a possible 11.97% upside.
  • Five-star Wells Fargo analyst Joseph Quatrochi reiterated a Buy rating for ASML stock and raised his price target to $1,450 from $1,140, implying a potential 14.74% upside for the shares.

Quatrochi's increased price target for ASML is tied to an update for the firm's bottom-up Semi industry model. He highlighted Industrial and Electrical leading AI indicators, AI token-driven supply/demand model, and WFE die-to-wafer models as reasons for the new price target.

To go along with all of this, semiconductor company TSMC (TSM) just reported incredibly strong earnings for Q4 2025. That's important to ASML as TSMC is one of its top customers.

ASML Stock Movement Today

ASML stock was up 6.57% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 0.51% drop yesterday. The shares have rallied 18.12% year-to-date and 68.43% over the past 12 months.

Despite the analysts' updates and earnings news, shares of ASML haven't seen much activity today. As of this writing, more than 240,000 shares have changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 1.3 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: TSM Stock Wins a New Street-High Price Target After Blockbuster Results
data.description:
Needham's top analyst Charles Shi raised his price target on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) after its blockbuster fourth quarter results and above-consensus guidance. Shi kept his Buy rating and lifted the price target from $360 to a new Street-high of $410, implying 25.3% upside potential from current levels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

TSM shares are rallying in pre-market trading after the contract chip manufacturer reported a 35% jump in Q4 profit, surpassing estimates, driven by robust demand for AI chips. For Q4FY25, TSM posted NT$505.74 billion ($16 billion) in net income and diluted EPS of NT$19.50 ($3.14 per ADR). Meanwhile, revenue increased 25.5% year-over-year to NT$1.046 trillion ($33.73 billion), beating the expected NT$1.034 trillion.

Shi is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #313 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 58% success rate and an average return per rating of 27.50%.

Analyst Raises Estimates on Bullish Outlook

Shi highlighted the following key upgrades in TSMC's guidance.

  • 2026 Revenue Growth: Projected near 30%, surpassing the Street's 25% estimate and his own prior 25% model.
  • 2026 CapEx: Expected at $52-$56 billion, above his prior $50 billion forecast.
  • AI Revenue CAGR (through 2029): Raised to mid-to-high 50%, from previous mid-40%.
  • Long-Term Revenue CAGR: Now "approaching" 25%, up from 20%.
  • Through-Cycle Gross Margin: Elevated to "56% and higher," from "53% and higher."

Shi noted that TSMC's updated outlook significantly outpaces prior Street consensus and his own model. In turn, he revised forecasts upward, projecting TSMC's total 2027 revenue to approach $200 billion, up from the previous $182 billion, and AI revenue to hit $120 billion (previously $90 billion). This justifies the new $410 price target amid robust AI-driven momentum.

Is TSM a Good Stock to Buy?

Analysts remain highly optimistic about Taiwan Semiconductor's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, TSMC has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on seven Buys and one Hold rating. The average Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing price target of $320.61 implies nearly 2% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSM shares have gained over 58%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Stock Futures Trade Mixed, Chip Stocks Rise on TSMC Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed on Thursday morning after Big Tech names dragged down major indexes in the previous trading session. Chip stocks trended higher after Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing or TSMC (TSM) reported impressive results. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.92% and 0.41%, respectively, while those on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.09%, at 8:42 a.m. EST on January 15.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed in the red, marking a second consecutive day of losses for them. The S&P 500 and the Dow Jones declined 0.53% and 0.09%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite fell 1.00%.

Yesterday, Chinese authorities told domestic firms to steer clear of around 12 U.S. and Israeli software vendors on national security grounds, leading to a slump in software stocks. Microsoft (MSFT), Meta (META), and Amazon (AMZN) each dropped more than 2%. Oracle (ORCL) and Broadcom each slid 4%. Moreover, NVDA (NVDA) shares dropped 1.4% after China instructed domestic firms to avoid H200 purchases. U.S. banks were also among the biggest laggards.

Turning to trending stocks, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing shares jumped about 7% in pre-market trading after the chip company reported a 35% jump in the fourth quarter profit, thanks to AI-led demand for its chips. Shares of ASML Holding (ASML), a chip equipment manufacturer and a supplier for TSMC, were up 6%. Also, Nvidia, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO) stocks were up 1.5%, 3.05%, and about 3%, respectively.

Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs (GS) stock fell more than 1% after the investment bank reported its fourth-quarter results. In contrast, Dell Technologies (DELL) stock was up nearly 2% as Barclays upgraded the tech stock to Buy from Hold, citing strength in AI server orders.

data.title: Here's Why Agape ATP Stock (ATPC) Rocketed Today
data.description:
Agape ATP (ATPC) stock underwent a massive rally on Thursday despite a lack of news from the health and wellness investment holding company. There are no press releases or filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) that explain why the shares are up today.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors will note that this isn't the first time that ATPC stock has seen major movement without a reason. A similar event happened in December, and it triggered a response from the company. At that time, the company said it wasn't "aware of any material, undisclosed corporate developments or adverse operating conditions that would explain the recent fluctuations in the Company's share price." The same is likely true today.

Instead, the recent interest in Agape ATP stock is possibly tied to retail traders. A group of these traders has likely decided to target ATPC stock as a pump-and-dump for quick gains. The recent volatility without a catalyst backs up this theory. That also means that traders will want to be careful about investing in ATPC alongside its recent rallies.

Agape ATP Stock Movement Today

Agape ATP stock was up 69.57% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 6.06% gain on Wednesday. Despite these gains, the stock has fallen 33.96% year-to-date and 94.53% over the past 12 months.

With today's rally comes increased trading activity for Agape ATP. This saw more than 231 million shares change hands this morning, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 43,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/15/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Mixed as Tech Stocks Slip, Indexes Fall
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were trading mixed early Thursday as losses in Big Tech names dragged down major indexes. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.23% and 0.06%, respectively, while those on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.09%, at 3:08 a.m. EST on January 15.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed in the red, marking a second consecutive day of losses for them. The S&P 500 declined 0.53%, the Dow dropped 0.09%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 1.00%.

Yesterday, Chinese authorities told domestic firms to steer clear of around 12 U.S. and Israeli software vendors on national security grounds, leading to a slump in software stocks. Microsoft (MSFT), Meta (META), and Amazon (AMZN) each dropped more than 2%. Oracle (ORCL) and Broadcom (AVGO) slid 4% each. Moreover, NVDA (NVDA) shares dropped 1.4% on news that China instructed domestic firms to avoid H200 purchases.

Banks were also among the biggest laggards. Wells Fargo (WFC) dropped 4.6%, dragging the broader market lower after reporting weaker-than-expected Q4 revenue. Citigroup (C) and Bank of America (BAC) both fell more than 3%.

On the economic front, reports on initial weekly jobless claims and retail sales will be released today.

On the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Goldman Sachs (GS), Morgan Stanley (MS), and BlackRock (BLK).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.14%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $59.92 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,601 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as markets focused on the ongoing meeting between U.S., Greenland, and Danish officials regarding President Donald Trump's interest in acquiring the Danish territory of Greenland.

Asia-Pacific Markets Fall

Asia-Pacific markets fell on January 15 due to the ongoing U.S.-China chip trade tensions.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.25%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 0.33%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.07%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei declined 0.42%, but the Topix gained 0.68%.

data.title: Your Next Smartphone Will Cost a Lot More –  Here's Why
data.description:
As the memory chip shortage continues to unfold, smartphone and PC prices are likely to rise in 2026 as the global shortage begins to affect consumer devices. The shortage is tied to rapid growth in AI data centers, which are absorbing a large share of the memory supply that was once used for phones and laptops. As a result, device makers are facing higher costs for essential components such as storage and memory, which is now forcing difficult pricing decisions.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nothing founder and CEO Carl Pei warned this week that the era of low-priced smartphones is ending. In a post on X titled "Why Your Next Smartphone Will Cost More," Pei said memory costs have already tripled in some cases. He explained that brands are now left with a simple choice: either raise prices or reduce device specs. According to Pei, price increases of 30% or more may be required on some models to keep current features. He also said Nothing will inevitably raise prices across its phone lineup, with the biggest impact expected on devices launching in early 2026 that use UFS 3.1 storage.

Pressure Spreads Across the Device Market

Meanwhile, the pressure is spreading across the wider device market. Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) CEO TM Roh said at CES 2026 that the company is facing one of the harshest memory pricing environments in years and that phone price changes may be needed. Samsung marketing head Wonjin Lee also said the company is reviewing possible repricing to reflect the new cost structure.

At the same time, PC makers are signaling similar moves. Dell Technologies (DELL) has warned customers of price increases of 15%-20%, while HP (HPQ) CEO Enrique Lores said the second half of 2026 could be especially difficult for pricing. Lenovo Group Limited (LNVGY) has already told customers that existing price quotes expired at the start of 2026 due to memory shortages and rising AI-related demands.

Looking at the broader impact, International Data Corporation estimates PC prices could rise by 4%-8%, while smartphone prices may increase by 3%-8%, depending on how tight supply becomes. In a weaker demand scenario, the firm expects global device shipments to fall by nearly 9%.

At the same time, TrendForce said smartphone production is now expected to decline 7% year over year in 2026, compared with an earlier forecast of a much smaller drop. IDC device research head Tom Mainelli said the shortage could hit smaller brands the hardest, adding that large vendors are likely to gain share while some regional players may struggle to stay in the market.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align all stocks mentioned in the text with other smartphone makers and chip manufacturers, which are also affected by the current landscape.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Elon Musk Ends FSD Buyouts: What the Subscription Push Means for Tesla Stock
data.description:
Tesla Inc. (TSLA) currently holds a staggering $1.5 trillion valuation, but beneath the high stock price, a shift in its business strategy suggests the company is bracing for a more competitive future. On Wednesday, January 14, 2026, CEO Elon Musk announced that Tesla will stop selling its Full Self-Driving (FSD) software as a one-time purchase after February 14. Instead, the technology will only be available through a monthly subscription model, a move that aligns with Musk's targets for his massive $1 trillion compensation package.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla Pushes for 10 Million Subscribers

The decision to kill the one-time purchase option is a tactical necessity for Elon Musk's personal compensation. In November 2025, investors approved a $1 trillion pay package for the CEO, but it comes with rigorous strings attached. One of the core requirements to unlock this award is for Tesla to reach 10 million active FSD subscriptions. Essentially, this removes one's ability to own the software for life because Tesla forces all future users into the recurring payment pool. This helps Musk hit his targets while providing Wall Street with the predictable income it craves.

Tesla's Biggest Threat May Be Nvidia

Tesla's biggest threat might not be another car company, but a chip company. At CES 2026, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang revealed Alpamayo, a "chain-of-thought" AI model that helps vehicles reason through complex driving situations. Huang described the launch as the "ChatGPT moment for physical AI," where machines finally begin to understand and act in the real world.

Because Nvidia is open-sourcing this technology, any automaker, from Mercedes (MBGAF) to Uber (UBER), can now build advanced self-driving features without spending the billions Tesla did on original research. This normalization of self-driving technology could potentially strip Tesla stock of the massive valuation premium it enjoys as the supposed leader in the field.

Tesla's Narrative Is Under Pressure

Tesla's valuation currently sits near $1.5 trillion, a number that assumes the company will eventually dominate the global robotaxi market. However, the data shows that Tesla's EV sales have actually fallen for two years in a row, with China's BYD (BYDDF) now selling more battery-electric cars than Tesla.

Analysts suggest that Tesla's primary defense is its narrative command, or Musk's ability to keep the public focused on the next big thing. Whether it is the move to subscriptions or the upcoming robotaxi launch in Austin, the goal is to keep the story of Tesla alive.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and eight Sells assigned in the last three months. At $394.12, the average Tesla price target implies a 10.26% downside risk.
data.author: Annika Masrani
